svg.text: Pruning — The Difference Between Tractable and Not
• Constraint propagation — drop choices that violate a constraint immediately
• Bound-based — stop if best-possible-from-here is worse than known best
• Symmetry — fix the first choice to break symmetric duplicates
• Skip duplicates — sort and skip same value at same depth
• Memoise on state hash — when state can repeat across branches
A good prune turns 10^10 branches into 10^4 reachable ones.
Mutable: shared list, mutate before recurse, undo after. O(1) per step.
Immutable: pass new copy each call. O(depth) per step but easier to reason.
For solution emit, snapshot the mutable list (current.copy()) before saving
— otherwise all stored solutions point to the same mutable buffer.
Mutable accumulator with snapshot-on-emit is the canonical performance choice.
Backtracking is DFS with disciplined state management.
